@graph.description:
We want to recreate the original CITE-seq proof-of-principle from Stoeckius et al. 2017 Nat Methods. The data is on GEO under GSE100866 and includes three experiments: a Drop-seq species-mixing assay (HeLa human + 4T1 mouse), the CBMC profiling experiment (~8,005 cord blood cells with a panel of 13 antibodies and ~4% mouse 3T3 fibroblast spike-in for ADT background calibration, on 10X Single Cell 3' v2), and a CD8a sorted-pool comparison between flow cytometry and CITE-seq.

Process the species-mixing data with Drop-seq tools v1.12 against a hybrid hg19+mm10 reference (load 400 cells/uL) and recreate the 97.2% agreement between transcriptomic and ADT species classification at the single-cell level (Fig 1d).

For the CBMC arm, process with Cell Ranger v1.2 against hg19 (NOT GRCh38) with default parameters. Total droplets = 8,617 (8,005 human + 579 mouse + 33 mixed after species classification). Cluster the cells unsupervised on RNA; the mouse 3T3 cells should appear as a distinct cluster representing ~4% of total cells. Use the 3T3 cluster's per-antibody ADT counts to set the signal-vs-background threshold per antibody. Three antibody-oligo conjugates are expected to fall below background and should be excluded -- report which three.

Subtract per-antibody background, then CLR-normalize ADT across cells. Recreate Fig 3a (cluster tSNE), Fig 3b (per-cluster ADT enrichment matrix recovering CD3e in T, CD4/CD8a in T subsets, CD19 in B, CD56/CD16 in NK, CD11c/CD14 in monocytes/DC, CD34 in the rare ~2% precursor cluster), and Fig 3c (CD4 vs CD8a, CD16 vs CD56, CD14 vs CD4 bi-axial gating plots).

Subdivide the NK cluster on CD56 ADT level into CD56-bright vs CD56-dim; verify CD16 ADT is higher in CD56-dim; compare 11 prior NK-subset DE genes (including GZMB, GZMK, PRF1) and reproduce the published direction in 10 of 11.

For the CD8a sorted-pool experiment, sort CBMCs on a Sony SH800 cell sorter into 4 pools by CD8a fluorescence (+++, ++, +, +/-), load each pool at 150 cells/uL on Drop-seq, then reanalyze each by both methods; recreate the linear quantitative agreement (Fig 2c-f).

No mechanistic claims -- descriptive multimodal QC only.
@graph.name:
computational biology — scRNA-seq clustering + differential expression. Standard pipeline:
preprocess, cell-level QC, normalise, optional batch correction +
integration sweep, dimensionality reduction, cluster, annotate cell
types, test cluster-vs-rest or cross-condition DE, then pathway
enrichment. Mirrors today's `config/stage-taxonomies/single-cell.yaml`.

Discover method for sequence_trimming (options: trimmomatic, cutadapt, fastp, trim_galore). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for quantification (options: salmon, kallisto, featurecounts, htseq, rsem). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for pathway_enrichment (options: fgsea, clusterprofiler, gsea, enrichr). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for normalisation (options: deseq2_vst, edger_tmm, scran, seurat_lognormalize, sctransform). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for dimensionality_reduction (options: pca, umap, tsne, phate). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for differential_expression (options: deseq2, edger, limma_voom, mast, wilcoxon). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for clustering (options: leiden, louvain, kmeans, hierarchical). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for cell_type_annotation (options: celltypist, singler, azimuth, sctype, marker_panel). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for batch_correction (options: harmony, scvi, bbknn, mnn_correct, combat, seurat_cca). Surface set_intake_method.
Discover method for alignment (options: star, hisat2, bwa_mem2, bwa_mem, minimap2, bowtie2). Surface set_intake_method.

Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Validate outputs of: Acquire raw experimental data from public repositories (SRA, GEO, ArrayExpress,
ENA, PRIDE, ClinVar, gnomAD, GTEx) or SME-supplied paths. Emits a cohort manifest,
per-accession provenance summary, and an index pointing at the materialized
artifacts. Downstream atoms consume the manifest, not the raw bytes directly.

Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Validate outputs of: Run sequencing QC (FastQC, MultiQC) on raw FASTQ inputs. Emits a per-sample metric
table and aggregated report; downstream `sequence_trimming` reads the trim-needed
flag.

Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Validate outputs of: Trim adapters and low-quality bases from raw reads. Conditional in legacy
taxonomies on `raw_qc.result.needs_trimming`; the atom always runs and emits
trimmed FASTQ regardless of whether it altered the inputs (idempotent on
already-clean data).

Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Validate outputs of: Align trimmed reads to a reference genome. Method neutrality: the concrete
aligner (STAR / HISAT2 / BWA-MEM2 / minimap2 / Bowtie2 / Salmon-alevin)
is selected at runtime by the agent. EDAM operation:3198 (Read mapping)
is the more-specific child of operation:0292 (Sequence alignment).

Quantify gene- or transcript-level expression from aligned reads. Method
neutrality: featureCounts / HTSeq / Salmon / kallisto / RSEM are selected
at runtime. Emits a count matrix consumed by downstream normalisation.

Validate outputs of: Quantify gene- or transcript-level expression from aligned reads. Method
neutrality: featureCounts / HTSeq / Salmon / kallisto / RSEM are selected
at runtime. Emits a count matrix consumed by downstream normalisation.

Apply count-matrix-level QC: cell or sample filtering by total counts, gene
detection, mitochondrial fraction, and doublet score. Distinct from `raw_qc`
(which operates on raw reads). swfc:scrnaseq_cell_qc per the EDAM audit —
EDAM operation:3218 conflates raw-read QC with count-matrix QC.

Validate outputs of: Apply count-matrix-level QC: cell or sample filtering by total counts, gene
detection, mitochondrial fraction, and doublet score. Distinct from `raw_qc`
(which operates on raw reads). swfc:scrnaseq_cell_qc per the EDAM audit —
EDAM operation:3218 conflates raw-read QC with count-matrix QC.

Standardise expression values to remove library-size and composition biases.
Method neutrality: DESeq2 VST / edgeR TMM / scran pooling / Seurat
LogNormalize / scnorm / sctransform are selected at runtime.

Validate outputs of: Standardise expression values to remove library-size and composition biases.
Method neutrality: DESeq2 VST / edgeR TMM / scran pooling / Seurat
LogNormalize / scnorm / sctransform are selected at runtime.

Remove technical batch effects between donors / runs / sequencing centres
while preserving biological signal. swfc namespace per EDAM audit —
operation:3435 conflates with normalisation.

Validate outputs of: Remove technical batch effects between donors / runs / sequencing centres
while preserving biological signal. swfc namespace per EDAM audit —
operation:3435 conflates with normalisation.

Sensitivity comparison across batch-correction methods (Harmony / scVI /
BBKNN / Scanorama). Materialises method-vs-method concordance metrics so
the SME can pick a winner via `select_sensitivity_winner`. Aggregator role
because it fans in multiple corrected matrices.

Validate outputs of: Sensitivity comparison across batch-correction methods (Harmony / scVI /
BBKNN / Scanorama). Materialises method-vs-method concordance metrics so
the SME can pick a winner via `select_sensitivity_winner`. Aggregator role
because it fans in multiple corrected matrices.

Project the (normalised, batch-corrected) feature matrix to a low-dimensional
embedding for visualization and clustering. PCA followed by UMAP or t-SNE is
the default; method choice deferred to runtime.

Validate outputs of: Project the (normalised, batch-corrected) feature matrix to a low-dimensional
embedding for visualization and clustering. PCA followed by UMAP or t-SNE is
the default; method choice deferred to runtime.

Group cells / samples by transcriptional similarity in the reduced embedding.
Leiden / Louvain / k-means / hierarchical methods selected at runtime.
Resolution sweep is part of the emitted artifact so downstream review can
re-pick without recomputation.

Validate outputs of: Group cells / samples by transcriptional similarity in the reduced embedding.
Leiden / Louvain / k-means / hierarchical methods selected at runtime.
Resolution sweep is part of the emitted artifact so downstream review can
re-pick without recomputation.

Label each cluster with a cell-identity from marker-gene panels or reference
mapping (CellTypist / SingleR / Azimuth / sctype). swfc namespace per the
EDAM audit — operation:0361 (sequence annotation) is a semantic mismatch
for cell-identity calling on count matrices.

Validate outputs of: Label each cluster with a cell-identity from marker-gene panels or reference
mapping (CellTypist / SingleR / Azimuth / sctype). swfc namespace per the
EDAM audit — operation:0361 (sequence annotation) is a semantic mismatch
for cell-identity calling on count matrices.

Test for differentially expressed genes between conditions / cell types.
DESeq2 / edgeR / limma-voom / MAST / Wilcoxon selected at runtime based on
modality (bulk vs single-cell) and design.

Validate outputs of: Test for differentially expressed genes between conditions / cell types.
DESeq2 / edgeR / limma-voom / MAST / Wilcoxon selected at runtime based on
modality (bulk vs single-cell) and design.

Test pre-defined gene sets (KEGG / Reactome / GO / MSigDB hallmark) against
ranked DE results. fgsea / clusterProfiler / GSEA / enrichr selected at runtime.

Validate outputs of: Test pre-defined gene sets (KEGG / Reactome / GO / MSigDB hallmark) against
ranked DE results. fgsea / clusterProfiler / GSEA / enrichr selected at runtime.

Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Validate outputs of: Assemble narrative tables, figures, and summary text from upstream stage
outputs into a per-stage HTML / Markdown report. Modality-agnostic; bio
taxonomies override the description at the include site to specify what
goes into the report.

Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.

Validate outputs of: Assemble the project-level final report: cross-stage narrative, dashboard
index, key figures, methods + caveats. Final node before SME review.
